speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I muse my lord of Gloucester is not come.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: ’Tis not his wont to be the hindmost man,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whate’er occasion keeps him from us now.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Can you not see, or will you not observe,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The strangeness of his altered countenance?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With what a majesty he bears himself,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: How insolent of late he is become,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: How proud, how peremptory, and unlike himself?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: We know the time since he was mild and affable;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And if we did but glance a far-off look,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Immediately he was upon his knee,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That all the court admired him for submission.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But meet him now, and, be it in the morn

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: When everyone will give the time of day,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: He knits his brow and shows an angry eye

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And passeth by with stiff unbowèd knee,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Disdaining duty that to us belongs.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Small curs are not regarded when they grin,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But great men tremble when the lion roars—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And Humphrey is no little man in England.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: First, note that he is near you in descent,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And, should you fall, he is the next will mount.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Meseemeth then it is no policy,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Respecting what a rancorous mind he bears

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And his advantage following your decease,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That he should come about your royal person

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or be admitted to your Highness’ Council.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: By flattery hath he won the Commons’ hearts;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And when he please to make commotion,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: ’Tis to be feared they all will follow him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Now ’tis the spring, and weeds are shallow-rooted;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Suffer them now, and they’ll o’ergrow the garden

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And choke the herbs for want of husbandry.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The reverent care I bear unto my lord

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Made me collect these dangers in the Duke.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If it be fond, call it a woman’s fear,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Which fear, if better reasons can supplant,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I will subscribe and say I wronged the Duke.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: My lords of Suffolk, Buckingham, and York,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Reprove my allegation if you can,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or else conclude my words effectual.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Well hath your Highness seen into this duke,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And, had I first been put to speak my mind,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I think I should have told your Grace’s tale.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The Duchess by his subornation,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Upon my life, began her devilish practices;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Or if he were not privy to those faults,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yet, by reputing of his high descent—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As next the King he was successive heir,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And such high vaunts of his nobility—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Did instigate the bedlam brainsick duchess

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: By wicked means to frame our sovereign’s fall.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And in his simple show he harbors treason.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The fox barks not when he would steal the lamb.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No, no, my sovereign, Gloucester is a man

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Unsounded yet and full of deep deceit.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Did he not, contrary to form of law,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Devise strange deaths for small offenses done?

speaker: YORK
text: And did he not, in his protectorship,

speaker: YORK
text: Levy great sums of money through the realm

speaker: YORK
text: For soldiers’ pay in France, and never sent it,

speaker: YORK
text: By means whereof the towns each day revolted?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Tut, these are petty faults to faults unknown,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which time will bring to light in smooth Duke Humphrey.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My lords, at once: the care you have of us

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To mow down thorns that would annoy our foot

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is worthy praise; but, shall I speak my conscience,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Our kinsman Gloucester is as innocent

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From meaning treason to our royal person

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As is the sucking lamb or harmless dove.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The Duke is virtuous, mild, and too well given

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To dream on evil or to work my downfall.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ah, what’s more dangerous than this fond affiance?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Seems he a dove? His feathers are but borrowed,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For he’s disposèd as the hateful raven.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is he a lamb? His skin is surely lent him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For he’s inclined as is the ravenous wolves.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Who cannot steal a shape that means deceit?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Take heed, my lord; the welfare of us all

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hangs on the cutting short that fraudful man.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: All health unto my gracious sovereign!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Welcome, Lord Somerset. What news from France?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: That all your interest in those territories

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Is utterly bereft you. All is lost.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Cold news, Lord Somerset; but God’s will be done.

speaker: YORK
text: Cold news for me, for I had hope of France

speaker: YORK
text: As firmly as I hope for fertile England.

speaker: YORK
text: Thus are my blossoms blasted in the bud,

speaker: YORK
text: And caterpillars eat my leaves away.

speaker: YORK
text: But I will remedy this gear ere long,

speaker: YORK
text: Or sell my title for a glorious grave.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: All happiness unto my lord the King!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Pardon, my liege, that I have stayed so long.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Nay, Gloucester, know that thou art come too soon,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Unless thou wert more loyal than thou art.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I do arrest thee of high treason here.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Well, Suffolk, thou shalt not see me blush

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Nor change my countenance for this arrest.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: A heart unspotted is not easily daunted.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The purest spring is not so free from mud

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As I am clear from treason to my sovereign.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Who can accuse me? Wherein am I guilty?

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis thought, my lord, that you took bribes of France

speaker: YORK
text: And, being Protector, stayed the soldiers’ pay,

speaker: YORK
text: By means whereof his Highness hath lost France.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Is it but thought so? What are they that think it?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I never robbed the soldiers of their pay

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Nor ever had one penny bribe from France.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: So help me God as I have watched the night—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, night by night—in studying good for England!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That doit that e’er I wrested from the King,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or any groat I hoarded to my use,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Be brought against me at my trial day!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: No, many a pound of mine own proper store,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Because I would not tax the needy Commons,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Have I dispursèd to the garrisons

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And never asked for restitution.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: It serves you well, my lord, to say so much.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I say no more than truth, so help me God.

speaker: YORK
text: In your protectorship, you did devise

speaker: YORK
text: Strange tortures for offenders, never heard of,

speaker: YORK
text: That England was defamed by tyranny.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Why, ’tis well known that whiles I was Protector,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Pity was all the fault that was in me;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For I should melt at an offender’s tears,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And lowly words were ransom for their fault.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Unless it were a bloody murderer

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or foul felonious thief that fleeced poor passengers,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I never gave them condign punishment.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Murder indeed, that bloody sin, I tortured

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Above the felon or what trespass else.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: My lord, these faults are easy, quickly answered;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But mightier crimes are laid unto your charge

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Whereof you cannot easily purge yourself.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I do arrest you in his Highness’ name,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And here commit you to my Lord Cardinal

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To keep until your further time of trial.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My lord of Gloucester, ’tis my special hope

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That you will clear yourself from all suspense.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My conscience tells me you are innocent.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ah, gracious lord, these days are dangerous.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Virtue is choked with foul ambition,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And charity chased hence by rancor’s hand;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Foul subornation is predominant,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And equity exiled your Highness’ land.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I know their complot is to have my life;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And if my death might make this island happy

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And prove the period of their tyranny,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I would expend it with all willingness.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But mine is made the prologue to their play;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For thousands more, that yet suspect no peril,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Will not conclude their plotted tragedy.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Beaufort’s red sparkling eyes blab his heart’s malice,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And Suffolk’s cloudy brow his stormy hate;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sharp Buckingham unburdens with his tongue

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The envious load that lies upon his heart;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And dogged York, that reaches at the moon,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Whose overweening arm I have plucked back,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: By false accuse doth level at my life.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And you, my sovereign lady, with the rest,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Causeless have laid disgraces on my head

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And with your best endeavor have stirred up

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My liefest liege to be mine enemy.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, all of you have laid your heads together—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Myself had notice of your conventicles—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And all to make away my guiltless life.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I shall not want false witness to condemn me

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Nor store of treasons to augment my guilt.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The ancient proverb will be well effected:

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: “A staff is quickly found to beat a dog.”

speaker: CARDINAL
text: My liege, his railing is intolerable.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: If those that care to keep your royal person

speaker: CARDINAL
text: From treason’s secret knife and traitor’s rage

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Be thus upbraided, chid, and rated at,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And the offender granted scope of speech,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: ’Twill make them cool in zeal unto your Grace.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Hath he not twit our sovereign lady here

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: With ignominious words, though clerkly couched,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As if she had subornèd some to swear

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: False allegations to o’erthrow his state?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But I can give the loser leave to chide.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Far truer spoke than meant. I lose, indeed;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Beshrew the winners, for they played me false!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And well such losers may have leave to speak.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He’ll wrest the sense and hold us here all day.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Lord Cardinal, he is your prisoner.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Sirs, take away the Duke, and guard him sure.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ah, thus King Henry throws away his crutch

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Before his legs be firm to bear his body.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thus is the shepherd beaten from thy side,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And wolves are gnarling who shall gnaw thee first.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ah, that my fear were false; ah, that it were!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For, good King Henry, thy decay I fear.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My lords, what to your wisdoms seemeth best

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do, or undo, as if ourself were here.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, will your Highness leave the Parliament?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, Margaret. My heart is drowned with grief,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose flood begins to flow within mine eyes,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My body round engirt with misery;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For what’s more miserable than discontent?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, uncle Humphrey, in thy face I see

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The map of honor, truth, and loyalty;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And yet, good Humphrey, is the hour to come

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That e’er I proved thee false or feared thy faith.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What louring star now envies thy estate

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That these great lords and Margaret our queen

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do seek subversion of thy harmless life?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou never didst them wrong nor no man wrong.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And as the butcher takes away the calf

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And binds the wretch and beats it when it strains,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Bearing it to the bloody slaughterhouse,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Even so remorseless have they borne him hence;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And as the dam runs lowing up and down,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Looking the way her harmless young one went,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And can do naught but wail her darling’s loss,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Even so myself bewails good Gloucester’s case

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With sad unhelpful tears, and with dimmed eyes

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Look after him and cannot do him good,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So mighty are his vowèd enemies.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His fortunes I will weep and, ’twixt each groan,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Say “Who’s a traitor, Gloucester he is none.”

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Free lords, cold snow melts with the sun’s hot beams.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Henry my lord is cold in great affairs,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Too full of foolish pity; and Gloucester’s show

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Beguiles him, as the mournful crocodile

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With sorrow snares relenting passengers,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or as the snake, rolled in a flow’ring bank,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With shining checkered slough, doth sting a child

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That for the beauty thinks it excellent.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Believe me, lords, were none more wise than I—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And yet herein I judge mine own wit good—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This Gloucester should be quickly rid the world,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To rid us from the fear we have of him.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: That he should die is worthy policy,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: But yet we want a color for his death.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: ’Tis meet he be condemned by course of law.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But, in my mind, that were no policy.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The King will labor still to save his life,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The Commons haply rise to save his life,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And yet we have but trivial argument,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: More than mistrust, that shows him worthy death.

speaker: YORK
text: So that, by this, you would not have him die.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Ah, York, no man alive so fain as I!

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis York that hath more reason for his death.

speaker: YORK
text: But, my Lord Cardinal, and you, my lord of Suffolk,

speaker: YORK
text: Say as you think, and speak it from your souls:

speaker: YORK
text: Were ’t not all one an empty eagle were set

speaker: YORK
text: To guard the chicken from a hungry kite

speaker: YORK
text: As place Duke Humphrey for the King’s Protector?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: So the poor chicken should be sure of death.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Madam, ’tis true; and were ’t not madness then

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To make the fox surveyor of the fold—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Who, being accused a crafty murderer,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: His guilt should be but idly posted over

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Because his purpose is not executed?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No, let him die in that he is a fox,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: By nature proved an enemy to the flock,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Before his chaps be stained with crimson blood,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As Humphrey, proved by reasons, to my liege.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And do not stand on quillets how to slay him—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Be it by gins, by snares, by subtlety,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Sleeping or waking. ’Tis no matter how,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So he be dead; for that is good deceit

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Which mates him first that first intends deceit.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thrice noble Suffolk, ’tis resolutely spoke.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Not resolute, except so much were done,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For things are often spoke and seldom meant;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But that my heart accordeth with my tongue,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Seeing the deed is meritorious,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And to preserve my sovereign from his foe,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Say but the word and I will be his priest.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: But I would have him dead, my lord of Suffolk,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Ere you can take due orders for a priest.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Say you consent and censure well the deed,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And I’ll provide his executioner.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: I tender so the safety of my liege.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Here is my hand. The deed is worthy doing.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And so say I.

speaker: YORK
text: And I. And now we three have spoke it,

speaker: YORK
text: It skills not greatly who impugns our doom.

speaker: POST
text: Great lords, from Ireland am I come amain

speaker: POST
text: To signify that rebels there are up

speaker: POST
text: And put the Englishmen unto the sword.

speaker: POST
text: Send succors, lords, and stop the rage betime,

speaker: POST
text: Before the wound do grow uncurable;

speaker: POST
text: For, being green, there is great hope of help.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: A breach that craves a quick expedient stop!

speaker: CARDINAL
text: What counsel give you in this weighty cause?

speaker: YORK
text: That Somerset be sent as regent thither.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis meet that lucky ruler be employed—

speaker: YORK
text: Witness the fortune he hath had in France.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: If York, with all his far-fet policy,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Had been the regent there instead of me,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: He never would have stayed in France so long.

speaker: YORK
text: No, not to lose it all, as thou hast done.

speaker: YORK
text: I rather would have lost my life betimes

speaker: YORK
text: Than bring a burden of dishonor home

speaker: YORK
text: By staying there so long till all were lost.

speaker: YORK
text: Show me one scar charactered on thy skin.

speaker: YORK
text: Men’s flesh preserved so whole do seldom win.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Nay, then, this spark will prove a raging fire

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If wind and fuel be brought to feed it with.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: No more, good York.—Sweet Somerset, be still.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy fortune, York, hadst thou been regent there,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Might happily have proved far worse than his.

speaker: YORK
text: What, worse than naught? Nay, then, a shame take all!

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And, in the number, thee that wishest shame!

speaker: CARDINAL
text: My lord of York, try what your fortune is.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Th’ uncivil kerns of Ireland are in arms

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And temper clay with blood of Englishmen.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: To Ireland will you lead a band of men,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Collected choicely, from each county some,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And try your hap against the Irishmen?

speaker: YORK
text: I will, my lord, so please his Majesty.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Why, our authority is his consent,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And what we do establish he confirms.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Then, noble York, take thou this task in hand.

speaker: YORK
text: I am content. Provide me soldiers, lords,

speaker: YORK
text: Whiles I take order for mine own affairs.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A charge, Lord York, that I will see performed.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But now return we to the false Duke Humphrey.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: No more of him, for I will deal with him,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: That henceforth he shall trouble us no more.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And so break off; the day is almost spent.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Lord Suffolk, you and I must talk of that event.

speaker: YORK
text: My lord of Suffolk, within fourteen days

speaker: YORK
text: At Bristow I expect my soldiers,

speaker: YORK
text: For there I’ll ship them all for Ireland.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll see it truly done, my lord of York.

speaker: YORK
text: Now, York, or never, steel thy fearful thoughts

speaker: YORK
text: And change misdoubt to resolution.

speaker: YORK
text: Be that thou hop’st to be, or what thou art

speaker: YORK
text: Resign to death; it is not worth th’ enjoying.

speaker: YORK
text: Let pale-faced fear keep with the mean-born man

speaker: YORK
text: And find no harbor in a royal heart.

speaker: YORK
text: Faster than springtime showers comes thought on thought,

speaker: YORK
text: And not a thought but thinks on dignity.

speaker: YORK
text: My brain, more busy than the laboring spider,

speaker: YORK
text: Weaves tedious snares to trap mine enemies.

speaker: YORK
text: Well, nobles, well, ’tis politicly done

speaker: YORK
text: To send me packing with an host of men.

speaker: YORK
text: I fear me you but warm the starvèd snake,

speaker: YORK
text: Who, cherished in your breasts, will sting your hearts.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Twas men I lacked, and you will give them me;

speaker: YORK
text: I take it kindly. Yet be well assured

speaker: YORK
text: You put sharp weapons in a madman’s hands.

speaker: YORK
text: Whiles I in Ireland nourish a mighty band,

speaker: YORK
text: I will stir up in England some black storm

speaker: YORK
text: Shall blow ten thousand souls to heaven or hell;

speaker: YORK
text: And this fell tempest shall not cease to rage

speaker: YORK
text: Until the golden circuit on my head,

speaker: YORK
text: Like to the glorious sun’s transparent beams,

speaker: YORK
text: Do calm the fury of this mad-bred flaw.

speaker: YORK
text: And for a minister of my intent,

speaker: YORK
text: I have seduced a headstrong Kentishman,

speaker: YORK
text: John Cade of Ashford,

speaker: YORK
text: To make commotion, as full well he can,

speaker: YORK
text: Under the title of John Mortimer.

speaker: YORK
text: In Ireland have I seen this stubborn Cade

speaker: YORK
text: Oppose himself against a troop of kerns,

speaker: YORK
text: And fought so long till that his thighs with darts

speaker: YORK
text: Were almost like a sharp-quilled porpentine;

speaker: YORK
text: And in the end being rescued, I have seen

speaker: YORK
text: Him caper upright like a wild Morisco,

speaker: YORK
text: Shaking the bloody darts as he his bells.

speaker: YORK
text: Full often, like a shag-haired crafty kern,

speaker: YORK
text: Hath he conversèd with the enemy,

speaker: YORK
text: And undiscovered come to me again

speaker: YORK
text: And given me notice of their villainies.

speaker: YORK
text: This devil here shall be my substitute;

speaker: YORK
text: For that John Mortimer, which now is dead,

speaker: YORK
text: In face, in gait, in speech he doth resemble.

speaker: YORK
text: By this, I shall perceive the Commons’ mind,

speaker: YORK
text: How they affect the house and claim of York.

speaker: YORK
text: Say he be taken, racked, and torturèd,

speaker: YORK
text: I know no pain they can inflict upon him

speaker: YORK
text: Will make him say I moved him to those arms.

speaker: YORK
text: Say that he thrive, as ’tis great like he will,

speaker: YORK
text: Why then from Ireland come I with my strength

speaker: YORK
text: And reap the harvest which that rascal sowed.

speaker: YORK
text: For, Humphrey being dead, as he shall be,

speaker: YORK
text: And Henry put apart, the next for me.
